headline: Conference of Rulers' deliberations on 4 key bills never disclosed
context: The Conference of Rulers considered four significant bills in 2025 including constitutional amendments, but deliberation details and voting records were not made public.

cards.big: Conference of Rulers vetted 4 major bills — deliberations completely secret
cards.sub: Constitutional body with legislative power operates without any transparency

cards.big: Article 38 gives Rulers Conference consent power over key legislation
cards.sub: Royal institution exercises legislative authority without public accountability norms

cards.big: 2 of 4 bills considered involved RM12 billion in fiscal commitments
cards.sub: Major spending decisions influenced by unaccountable deliberation process

cards.big: Thailand's Privy Council publishes advisory opinions on constitutional matters
cards.sub: Even monarchies with stronger royal power provide more transparency than Malaysia

cards.big: Constitutional power without transparency creates an accountability gap that democracy cannot fill
Should Conference of Rulers' legislative deliberations be disclosed after royal assent?
